citations.narrative:
On June 3, 2025, two dogs were tendered for transport by an intermediate handler at the American Airlines Cargo
Terminal in Los Angeles, CA. During the acceptance process, the intermediate handler placed a slip-collar leash on both
animals and removed them from their kennels for security and screening purposes. The intermediate handler maintained
control of one dog while handing the leash of the other dog to an airline employee. After the screening was completed, the
intermediate handler placed the first dog back into its kennel. The second dog slipped free from the restraint and ran
loose within the cargo warehouse. The dog eluded capture, bit one employee and exited the building toward the airfield.
The dog crossed two active runways and bit a second person before it was recovered. The animal received veterinary
care for injuries (avulsion to the paw pad and a torn dewclaw) sustained during the incident.
Failure to handle animals in a manner that maintains adequate control can result in physical harm or may have a negative
impact on the health and well-being of the animal, as well as create a risk to public safety.
Handling of all animals shall be done as expeditiously and carefully as possible in a manner that does not cause trauma,
behavioral stress, physical harm, or unnecessary discomfort. Corrective measures were implemented by the time of
inspection on March 24, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.131(b)(1)  Critical
   
Handling of animals. 
On June 3, 2025, two dogs were tendered for transport by an intermediate handler at the American Airlines Cargo 
Terminal in Los Angeles, CA. During the acceptance process, the intermediate handler placed a slip-collar leash on both 
animals and removed them from their kennels for security and screening purposes. The intermediate handler maintained 
control of one dog while handing the leash of the other dog to an airline employee. After the screening was completed, the 
intermediate handler placed the first dog back into its kennel. The second dog slipped free from the restraint and ran 
loose within the cargo warehouse. The dog eluded capture, bit one employee and exited the building toward the airfield. 
The dog crossed two active runways and bit a second person before it was recovered. The animal received veterinary 
care for injuries (avulsion to the paw pad and a torn dewclaw) sustained during the incident. 
 
Failure to handle animals in a manner that maintains adequate control can result in physical harm or may have a negative 
impact on the health and well-being of the animal, as well as create a risk to public safety. 
 
Handling of all animals shall be done as expeditiously and carefully as possible in a manner that does not cause trauma, 
behavioral stress, physical harm, or unnecessary discomfort. Corrective measures were implemented by the time of 
inspection on March 24, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
